commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6a5e0e620fe0a8065a5803a8ccc82c36b075f96d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR restores bounded prompt continuity projection for Codex app-server fresh/transient starts while keeping native resume history empty, with focused regression test updates.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +33. Total +50 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main empties Codex model-input history, transient/fresh lifecycle paths use `thread/start`, and `turn/start` receives only `promptText` unless a projection rewrites it. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor-visible proof update and a hook-ordering repair; ClawSweeper should not open an automated repair job while real behavior proof must come from the contributor's setup.

target.title: [codex] fix Codex fresh-thread continuity projection
target.body:
## Summary

- restore bounded continuity projection for Codex app-server turns that start a fresh thread without an active context-engine projection
- keep native Codex resumes on empty parallel history so mirrored OpenClaw transcript is not duplicated into resumed native threads
- cover the fresh-start, restricted transient-start, and stale thread-bootstrap no-context-engine paths with focused regression tests

## Root cause

PR #88262 correctly stopped passing the mirrored OpenClaw transcript as parallel Codex model history for normal native resumes, but it also removed the separate prompt-level continuity projection that fresh or transient `thread/start` turns still need when no active context engine is projecting context.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts -t "fresh-thread continuity|without a native thread binding|stale thread-bootstrap|native-disabled transient|dynamic tools unavailable|context-engine"`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server fresh or transient `thread/start` turns were dropping prior visible session context after #88262 when no active context engine projection existed.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the Codex app-server test harness exercising an existing binding plus `toolsAllow: []` restricted-runtime transient start path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts -t "fresh-thread continuity|without a native thread binding|stale thread-bootstrap|native-disabled transient|dynamic tools unavailable|context-engine"`

Evidence after fix: Terminal verification passed with `Test Files 4 passed` and `Tests 31 passed | 99 skipped (130)`. The covered transient-start assertions now require `thread/start` plus `turn/start` input containing `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn:`, the prior visible messages, and `Current user request:` for both the restricted `toolsAllow: []` transient path and the stale thread-bootstrap no-context-engine fresh-start path.

Observed result after fix: Fresh/transient Codex starts keep bounded continuity in the prompt, while the resume-path regression checks still confirm resumed native threads do not inject mirrored history.

What was not tested: No packaged app-server binary or remote Crabbox lane; this proof stays at the local harness-backed app-server path for the touched Codex runtime surface.

Closes #88352.
target.author: nianjiuzst
